reason: DOMAIN INCONSISTENCY between Definition 1 and Definition 2: Definition 1 (line 47) defines a timelike worldline as gamma: I -> R^4 where I is an OPEN INTERVAL in R. Definition 2 (line 65) then states 'Given N timelike worldlines gamma_j: R -> R^4' -- requiring the domain to be all of R, not just an open interval I. The proof of Proposition 1 (line 90) refers to gamma_j as a map from I, and the properness argument requires the worldline to eventually leave any compact set (needed for boundary terms in the conservation proof at line 397-399). If the worldline is defined on a bounded interval I=(a,b), then (i) gamma^0 need not be a diffeomorphism onto all of R (only onto gamma^0(I)), and (ii) properness could fail because the preimage of a compact set could be a non-compact subset of (a,b) that accumulates at an endpoint. The fix is either: (a) require I=R in Definition 1, (b) require gamma to be a proper embedding in Definition 1, or (c) add the hypothesis 'defined on R' in Proposition 1. As written, Proposition 1 inherits Definition 1's generality but the proof only works for I=R.
